growth_records.cite: Kulacki, Konrad J. et al. Effects of Nano-Titanium Dioxide on Freshwater Algal Population Dynamics. PLoS ONE (2012). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0047130. [PMC3468504]
growth_records.snippet: [Oscillatoria spp. GRmax-vs-nTiO2 intercept = 0.150 day-1]. The maximum specific growth rate (day-1) was calculated as the largest proportional increase in biomass measured between any two consecutive sampling days.
growth_records.notes: Oscillatoria spp. is a cyanobacterium (prokaryote). Control-condition (0 mg/L n-TiO2) maximum specific growth rate = regression intercept of GRmax-vs-[n-TiO2] = 0.150 day-1 (Table 2). Converted: 0.150/24 = 0.00625 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.150 = 4.62 day = 110.9 h. Fluorescence/chlorophyll-a-based, genus-level, single replicate, regression intercept -> low confidence. No uptake/secretion rates measured.
